name: ELDEN RING
description: THE CRITICALLY ACCLAIMED FANTASY ACTION RPG. Rise, Tarnished, and be guided by grace to brandish the power of the Elden Ring and become an Elden Lord in the Lands Between.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 10
  • Processor: INTEL CORE I5-8400 or AMD RYZEN 3 3300X
  • Memory: 12 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GEFORCE GTX 1060 3 GB or AMD RADEON RX 580 4 GB
  • DirectX: Version 12
  • Storage: 60 GB available space
  • Sound Card: Windows Compatible Audio Device
  • Additional Notes:
comment1: This game is very addictive and it is huge! It took me 275 hours to play and now I have started the DLC. One of the best games I have ever played, and I have been playing games for 50 years!
comment2:
[h3]A generational game, but not without flaws[/h3]

To preface this review: my only experience with any souls-like was 'The First Berserker: Khazan', and I never finished it.
Actually, I almost never finished Elden Ring either. A few hours in, and I was ready to quit, but the sheer number of players glazing this game was bugging me. "What do they see, that i dont?''
I couldn't tell you exactly when I found my answer; it was more of a gradual experience. But I do now understand, even if I don't fully agree.

The [b][i]world[/i][/b] that's built here is amazing. The ambience and overall vibes made me feel like this was a living, breathing realm full of despair and danger. Even without many cut scenes, I could still grasp the severity and gravity of the events that had taken place. (even if most of the time i had no idea what was happening in the story)

The [b][i]combat[/i][/b], while at times was a little frustrating, is still one of its best features. The build diversity and scope are something special that I can't really find a comparison to in anything else I've played before it. 
The bosses, too, were imaginative and challenging, and more than a few times I found myself jumping out of my seat and slamming my desk in excitement after I defeated them.

There's more I could praise, but I won't, because so many reviews have already done that. So instead ill bring up a few of the [b][i]negatives[/i][/b] that i dont see mentioned very often.
The target locking in this game is dreadful. I swear I died more times to this than actual bosses. It's mostly fine when you have a single enemy to deal with, but as soon as there are multiple targets, it fails miserably.
The other massive flaw I feel this game has is with its in-game systems/mechanics. I can't think of a single game I've ever played that required me to tab out and Google as many times as this. It really was ridiculous. There is just so much that isn't explained or explained well, and most players will miss out on a lot of content or waste a lot of time without Alt-tabbing and turning to guides. 

Overall though, I'm happy I put the time in and finished it. It was a great adventure.

⠀
comment3: Bought it late. Wish I hadn't waited. Played more hours than I can justify. Completed everything and immediately wanted to start again. The exploration alone is worth the price. Best gaming experience I've ever had. Just buy it. Explore slowly. Trust your curiosity. 10/10.
